title: clerodendrum johnstonii subsp. johnstonii
intro: Clerodendrum johnstonii subsp. johnstonii, also known as the Johnston's glory bower, is a plant species in the family Lamiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Clerodendrum johnstonii subsp. johnstonii include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it may also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
